settings.title: Ten Years at the Same Address
settings.excerpt: We signed the lease with one rented oven and a recipe book that belonged to a grandmother.
settings.body:
This summer Saffron turns ten, all of it spent in the same small room on the same street.

We opened with one tired oven, a handful of tables, and a stack of family recipes written by hand. A few of those dishes are still on the menu, more or less unchanged.

Ten years in one small room

We never moved to a bigger place, and that was a choice. The room is small enough that we know most faces by their second visit, and the kitchen is close enough to the tables that you can smell the spices blooming from your seat. That tired first oven was replaced years ago, but the dishes it cooked are still here. The grandmother whose handwriting fills our recipe book would recognise half the menu, which is exactly how we wanted it.

Staying put has its own rewards. We have watched regulars go from first dates to family tables, and we have cooked for some of the same people across the whole decade. That continuity is the thing we are quietly proudest of.

For the anniversary week

  • We are bringing back our very first menu for one week in June.
  • Dishes are priced the way they were a decade ago.
  • Several originals never left, so you can taste what has lasted.

To mark it, we are bringing back our very first menu for one week in June, priced the way it was a decade ago. Come see what dinner cost in our first year, and thank you for ten of them.
